$description: Tag / chip component tokens — outlined, uppercase category labels. The deliberate inverse of the badge: where the badge is a filled pill for status, the tag is a square-cornered (radius.sm), transparent-filled, bordered label for skills, categories, and metadata. Two emphasis levels map to neutral semantic foreground roles; both text pairings are verified WCAG AA on the base canvas.

tag.$description: Outlined uppercase tag/chip. Square corners and a transparent fill distinguish it from the filled pill badge. Use for non-interactive skill, category, or metadata labels — not status.

default.$description: Neutral tag — tertiary emphasis for metadata and category labels.

background.$description: Transparent fill — the tag reads as an outlined label, not a solid chip.

foreground.$description: Tertiary text — foreground.muted (#8B9683). Contrast vs canvas: 6.3:1 AA.

border.$description: Standard UI edge — border.default. Integrates quietly with the surrounding layout.

strong.$description: Emphasized tag — secondary emphasis for labels that should assert themselves (e.g. skill tags).

background.$description: Transparent fill — outlined label, same as default.

foreground.$description: Secondary text — foreground.alt (#A0A89A). Contrast vs canvas: 8.0:1 AA.

border.$description: Muted-foreground border — a touch more prominent than the default UI edge.

radius.$description: Small 4px radius — square-cornered, the inverse of the badge's full pill. Signals a label/category rather than a status pill.

x.$description: Horizontal padding — 8px. Tighter than the badge pill; suits compact uppercase labels.

y.$description: Vertical padding — 4px. Keeps the tag compact; height is driven by font size.
